acceptance_criteria: Architect output schema in plan-level.yaml includes `research_needs[]` with documented item shape.
Architect prompt and agent-guidance doc explain when to emit `research_needs` vs `open_questions`.
Architect-prompt-reentry tests assert the new field is present and defaults to empty array.
Workflow behavior unchanged when `research_needs` is empty (no new routes triggered yet).
Build passes with zero errors and warnings.
description: Extend the architect step's output schema in [.conductor/registry/workflows/plan-level.yaml](.conductor/registry/workflows/plan-level.yaml) with a `research_needs[]` array (items: `topic`, `question`, `why_it_matters`, optional `suggested_sources`). Update [.conductor/registry/prompts/architect-plan-level.md](.conductor/registry/prompts/architect-plan-level.md) and [.polyphony-config/agent-guidance/architect.md](.polyphony-config/agent-guidance/architect.md) so architects know when to emit `research_needs` vs. `open_questions` (research = "I'll investigate then re-plan"; open question = "I need a human decision"). Update the existing schema-shape unit/contract tests in [tests/architect-prompt-reentry.Tests.ps1](tests/architect-prompt-reentry.Tests.ps1) to cover the new field with a default empty array. Pure schema/prompt change — no routing yet; emitting the field must be a no-op in workflow behavior.
title: Architect output schema + prompt: emit `research_needs`

acceptance_criteria: New steps `research_needs_counter` and (when needed) `research_cap_gate` exist in `plan-level.yaml` and follow the structural pattern of the open_questions triad.
Architect → research sub-workflow → architect re-entry path skips `open_questions_policy`, the plan PR steps, and `plan_reviewer`.
`max_research_loops` is sourced from policy with a sensible default and respected by the counter.
Cap-reached path surfaces a human gate; no infinite loop is reachable from a misbehaving architect.
Findings document path from #3109 is exposed to the next architect iteration via a documented context channel.
Conductor workflow lint/validate passes; build passes with zero errors and warnings.
description: Add routing in [.conductor/registry/workflows/plan-level.yaml](.conductor/registry/workflows/plan-level.yaml) so that when `architect.output.research_needs | length > 0`, control flows into the research sub-workflow from #3109 and then re-enters `architect`, bypassing `open_questions_policy`, the plan PR steps, and `plan_reviewer` entirely. Mirror the existing `open_questions_counter` pattern to add a `research_needs_counter` that computes `iteration` and `cap_reached` against a `max_research_loops` policy value (default 3, surfaced via the same `policy_resolver` mechanism used by `open_questions_policy`). When `cap_reached`, route to a new `research_cap_gate` (human_gate) that mirrors the cap behavior of `open_questions_gate`. Thread the findings document path produced by #3109 into the next architect iteration via the same context channel `ancestor_chain` uses (or document the chosen channel in the YAML comment header).
title: plan-level.yaml routing: research sub-workflow invocation, review-bypass re-entry, bounded loop

acceptance_criteria: `bypass` scenario passes and asserts plan-review nodes were skipped.
`bound` scenario passes and asserts cap_gate fires at the expected iteration.
End-to-end demo notes are captured and linked from the Issue close-out comment.
All existing harness scenarios still pass.
Build passes with zero errors and warnings.
description: Add two scenarios under [tests/harness/scenarios/](tests/harness/scenarios/) using the existing `FakeProvider` pattern: (1) **bypass** — script an architect that emits a single `research_needs` entry on its first turn and a clean plan on its second; assert the research sub-workflow ran, assert `open_questions_policy`/plan-PR/`plan_reviewer` nodes did NOT run, and assert the second architect invocation received the findings document. (2) **bound** — script an architect that emits `research_needs` on every turn; assert the workflow halts at `research_cap_gate` after `max_research_loops` iterations and does not invoke the research sub-workflow further. Record an end-to-end dogfood demo (notes + run id) on a real planning iteration once #3108 and #3109 are merged.
title: Harness scenarios + end-to-end demo: bypass and bound
